frameworks.name: Bayesian causal inference
frameworks.one_line: rational observer attributes outcomes to either stable distribution or hidden intervening causes

frameworks.name: valence-dependent learning asymmetry
frameworks.one_line: asymmetric learning from good vs bad outcomes recast as causal attribution

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: outcome-driven belief updating background against which asymmetries arise

core_question: Do beliefs about hidden causes of outcomes produce valence-dependent asymmetries in learning?
key_assumptions: rational observers assign blame or credit to hidden causes when present
outcomes are generated by either a stable distribution or hidden agents
causal structure of the environment shapes belief updating
manipulations: explicit behavior of hidden agents (generating good vs bad outcomes)
two experiments with differing sample sizes
key_findings: participants learned less from bad outcomes when hidden causes likely generated them
the asymmetry reversed when hidden causes likely generated good outcomes
both directions of learning asymmetry arose in the same paradigm
interpretations: valence-dependent learning asymmetries are partly driven by causal attribution
causal beliefs provide a mechanistic framework for biased learning
